model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: I’m taking UConn because UCLA’s main edge is a strong, efficient offense, but their biggest swing piece (Tyler Bilodeau) has been managing a knee strain, and any limitation lowers UCLA’s scoring reliability against UConn’s size/physicality. UConn also entered the tournament with some injury questions of its own, but the Huskies’ typical tournament profile—rim protection, rebounding, and half-court defense—gives them a steadier path to win if this turns into a grind. UCLA absolutely has a credible upset path if Bilodeau is truly full-go and the Bruins’ shot-making carries, so confidence stays moderate.

reasoningStep.title: East R32: UCLA vs UConn — lean UConn on physicality/defensive floor with UCLA health a swing factor
reasoningStep.summary: Both teams have plausible win paths, but UCLA’s recent Bilodeau knee issue introduces more downside to their offensive baseline, nudging the pick toward UConn in a likely half-court, physical game.
evidence: UCLA ratings: KenPom #27 (AdjEM +21.64; AdjO 123.8 #22; AdjD 102.1), Torvik #24 (AdjO rank 17; AdjD rank 49).

ESPN reported UCLA leading scorer Tyler Bilodeau has a mild right knee strain but is expected to be available for the NCAA tournament (https://www.espn.com/mens-college-basketball/story/_/id/48206390/ucla-tyler-bilodeau-knee-strain-expected-ncaa-tournament).

CT Insider reported UConn had injury questions around Jaylin Stewart and Silas Demary Jr. entering the tournament (https://www.ctinsider.com/sports/uconn-mens-basketball/article/injury-news-dan-hurley-silas-demary-jaylin-stewart-22080303.php).
Matchup logic: if Bilodeau is limited, UCLA’s half-court scoring margin shrinks; UConn’s size/rebounding/defensive profile is a steadier path in a slower, physical game.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: UConn's Dan Hurley gives injury updates before NCAA Tournament Ct Insider Logo Hearst Newspapers Logo Skip to main content Subscribe Home Contact Us Advertise With Us Newsletters FAQ Privacy Notice Terms of Use E-Edition Our Brands Cheshire Herald Connecticut Magazine County Times Fairfield Citizen Journal Inquirer New Milford Spectrum Record-Journal Shoreline Times West Hartford News Westport News Waterbury Republican-American News Education Politics Election Health Latino News Sports GametimeCT UConn UConn Women's Basketball UConn Men's Basketball College Connecticut Sun WNBA NFL NBA MLB Hockey Business Entertainment Horoscopes Advice Puzzles Comics Real Estate Living Food Weather Opinion We Asked, You Answered Obits Best Of Education Home Services Legal Services Senior Living Entertainment Food & Drink Health Advertising Features Events Jobs Classifieds Ct Insider Logo Subscribe Trending: Speed cameras on highways? | Gusty winds for St Patrick's Day | CT power outages | CT pension spending | Horoscopes Sports // UConn Men's Basketball Dan Hurley gives injury updates on UConn's Jaylin Stewart, Silas Demary Jr. ahead of NCAA Tournament By Declan Walsh , Staff Writer March 16, 2026 STORRS, CONNECTICUT - NOVEMBER 19: Silas Demary Jr. #2 and Jaylin Stewart #3 of the Connecticut Huskies dive for the loose ball against Brayden Burries #5 of the Arizona Wildcats during the first half of an NCAA men's basketball game. Joe Buglewicz/Getty Images The UConn men's basketball team prepares for the upcoming NCAA Tournament potentially low on manpower with the injury statuses of two key contributors still in question ahead of Friday's opener against Furman. Jaylin Stewart has not played since Feb. 21, as the junior wing continues to recover from an unspecified knee injury, while Silas Demary Jr. was forced to exit Saturday's Big East Tournament final with a left ankle sprain . Dan Hurley provided updates on both players while speaking with the media Sunday evening. Advertisement Article continues below this ad "Health-wise, it will be close with Jaylin," Hurley said. The UConn coach provided a more promising update on his star point guard: "I think Silas is in pretty good shape." Jaylin Stewart has faced a mercurial junior season, starting UConn's first 10 games this season as freshman wing Braylon Mullins battled a pre-season injury — the Seattle native delivered double-digit scoring performances against Arizona and Texas. Stewart returned to an off-the-bench role once Mullins hit his stride around the New Year, but he stepped up with a crucial pair of 3-pointers against Villanova in his final game before the ongoing injury saga. More For You UConn stars Sarah Strong, Azzi Fudd named to USBWA All-America team The team is selected by members of the United States Basketball Writers Association. You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
